headline: Satya Nadella Has Positioned Microsoft to Grow. Investors Are Missing the Point.
summary: When Satya Nadella became  Microsoft  CEO in 2014, the software company was stuck in the mud.  Its Windows and Office franchises had matured, and forays into consumer devices and mobile operating systems had failed.  Nadella, 58, regained investor confidence with a shift to the cloud and a less cutthroat culture.

Nadella, 58, regained investor confidence with a shift to the cloud and a less cutthroat culture.

headline: The Week in Numbers: SpaceX's colossal climb, Pizza Hut's price tag
summary:
STORY:  From SpaceX's colossal climb up the market...to Pizza Hut's price tag

This is the week in numbers.

:: The Week In Numbers

:: $2.52 trln

$2.52 trillion was the market cap given to SpaceX as shares of the rocket maker soared this week.

Elon Musk's company's value catapulted past Amazon's and briefly above Microsoft's.

Towards the end of the week though, shares dropped more than 6%...

as the post-IPO frenzy that briefly placed Elon Musk's rockets-to-AI firm...

among the world's top five most valuable companies, appeared to fizzle out.

:: 30%

30% is how much Allbirds shares soared by after the former footwear company changed its name to Smartbird.

The move cemented its months-long transformation from shoemaker to AI infrastructure firm, shifting its focus to offer cloud computing capacity.

The company also appointed former Amazon executive Nadia Carlsten as its new CEO. 

:: 7%

More than 7% is the hit BMW's shares took after the German automaker issued a profit warning.

The premium carmaker blamed ongoing weakness in the world's largest auto market China, and the impact of the Iran war on prices and customer sentiment.

Analysts said the outlook cut was significantly larger than expected.

BMW shares dipped to their lowest level since 2020.

:: $22 bln 

$22 billion is the amount media giant Fox is spending on buying streaming platform Roku.

The deal gives the parent company of Fox News access to Roku's more than 100 million households.

Potentially helping the legacy cable and broadcast company better compete for eyeballs, target its ads and reduce reliance on traditional distribution. 

:: $2.7 bln

$2.7 billion is the price tag Yum brands set for Pizza Hut as the fast-food business struggles with stiff competition and cautious consumer spending.

Rising inflation and higher commodity costs have weighed on U.S. pizza chains facing weak demand.

Growing use of weight-loss drugs is nudging consumers toward healthier options.

headline: Amazon points to water conservation steps in India amid data centre scrutiny
summary: Amazon said on Friday its Indian operations had reached a major milestone in ‌water conservation, at a time when global tech giants face increasing pressure ‌over their expansion of resource-hungry AI data centres.  The U.S.-based company announced it had turned "water positive" in ​India this year - meaning it returns more water to communities than it uses across its operations, which include data centres, corporate offices and warehouses.  Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet's Google are among companies that are facing shareholder and activist pushback ⁠over the environmental impact of data centre projects, Reuters reported earlier this year.

content.title: Amazon points to water conservation steps in India amid data centre scrutiny
content.summary: Amazon said on Friday its Indian operations had reached a major milestone in ‌water conservation, at a time when global tech giants face increasing pressure ‌over their expansion of resource-hungry AI data centres.  The U.S.-based company announced it had turned "water positive" in ​India this year - meaning it returns more water to communities than it uses across its operations, which include data centres, corporate offices and warehouses.  Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet's Google are among companies that are facing shareholder and activist pushback ⁠over the environmental impact of data centre projects, Reuters reported earlier this year.

headline: Tech Weekly: A blockbuster IPO, a sweeping online block
summary:
STORY: From a record-breaking IPO to a far-reaching online ban, this is Tech Weekly. 

:: Tech Weekly

SpaceX on Thursday priced the biggest-ever U.S. initial public offering, raising a record $75 billion. 

It’s made Elon Musk’s rocket and spacecraft manufacturer one of the world’s most valuable companies.

Selling 555.56 million shares at $135 apiece, the company was valued at $1.77 trillion. 

Thursday’s pricing supports Musk's most ambitious project yet - even as some analysts question whether its lofty valuation is justified.

The company said Monday its underwriters had exercised the so-called "greenshoe" option to purchase additional shares.

Microsoft has been sued by shareholders who say they've been defrauded. 

They’ve said the tech giant inflated its stock price by failing to disclose slowing growth in its Azure cloud business.

And failing to disclose the need to spend billions of dollars on AI infrastructure. 

The proposed class action, filed Friday, was prompted by Microsoft shares falling 10% on January 29 after an earnings report.

About $357 billion of market value was erased, and Microsoft's stock suffered its biggest one-day decline in nearly six years.

Microsoft said on Monday it believes the claims are "without merit”. 

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday he would ban social media sites for under-16s..

And impose restrictions on gaming and livestreaming platforms for the age group. 

This amounts to some of the world's most far-reaching online restrictions, with the ban coming into place by next spring.

He said this would help protect children online while pushing back against the power of big technology companies.

"At the moment, strangers can contact any child unchecked. Just think about that. Is there a situation in the offline world where you would just let your child pair up with a stranger, an adult that you don't know anything about?”

Ford and other automakers are scrambling to obtain U.S. government authorization to continue selling certain models - despite being on the market for years. 

It’s due to a U.S. ban on Chinese-developed and -maintained software in connected vehicles. 

Ford told Reuters it’s asked the U.S. Commerce Department for authorization to continue importing its China-built Lincoln Nautilus SUV.

And said despite its tech being built in the U.S., its installation in China now requires government approval. 

Lawmakers have ​proposed making the rules even tougher.

:: Tech Weekly

SpaceX on Thursday priced the biggest-ever U.S. initial public offering, raising a record $75 billion. 

It’s made Elon Musk’s rocket and spacecraft manufacturer one of the world’s most valuable companies.

Selling 555.56 million shares at $135 apiece, the company was valued at $1.77 trillion. 

Thursday’s pricing supports Musk's most ambitious project yet - even as some analysts question whether its lofty valuation is justified.

The company said Monday its underwriters had exercised the so-called "greenshoe" option to purchase additional shares.

Microsoft has been sued by shareholders who say they've been defrauded. 

They’ve said the tech giant inflated its stock price by failing to disclose slowing growth in its Azure cloud business.

And failing to disclose the need to spend billions of dollars on AI infrastructure. 

The proposed class action, filed Friday, was prompted by Microsoft shares falling 10% on January 29 after an earnings report.

About $357 billion of market value was erased, and Microsoft's stock suffered its biggest one-day decline in nearly six years.

Microsoft said on Monday it believes the claims are "without merit”. 

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday he would ban social media sites for under-16s..

And impose restrictions on gaming and livestreaming platforms for the age group. 

This amounts to some of the world's most far-reaching online restrictions, with the ban coming into place by next spring.

He said this would help protect children online while pushing back against the power of big technology companies.

"At the moment, strangers can contact any child unchecked. Just think about that. Is there a situation in the offline world where you would just let your child pair up with a stranger, an adult that you don't know anything about?”

It’s due to a U.S. ban on Chinese-developed and -maintained software in connected vehicles. 

Ford told Reuters it’s asked the U.S. Commerce Department for authorization to continue importing its China-built Lincoln Nautilus SUV.

And said despite its tech being built in the U.S., its installation in China now requires government approval. 

Lawmakers have ​proposed making the rules even tougher.
